documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/Th0h0/autossrf.git; pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python3 autossrf.py -h`. Further invocations from upstream: `usage: autossrf.py [-h] [--file FILE] [--url URL] [--output] [--verbose]`; `python3 autossrf.py -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. autoSSRF is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: **autoSSRF** is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: autoSSRF is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use autossrf because **autoSSRF** is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following .
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 autossrf.py -h` as documented upstream for autossrf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `usage: autossrf.py [-h] [--file FILE] [--url URL] [--output] [--verbose]` as documented upstream for autossrf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 autossrf.py -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for autossrf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 autossrf.py -f urls.txt -v` as documented upstream for autossrf.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair autossrf with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `autossrf --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
